faqSection.label: FAQ
faqSection.title: Common
faqSection.q1: We just use ChatGPT — do we need this?
faqSection.a1: Yes. EU AI Act has specific obligations for deployers (companies that use AI tools). If your team uses ChatGPT, Copilot, or Midjourney — you have legal obligations under Articles 4, 26, and 27.
faqSection.q2: We’re a US company. Why do we need EU compliance?
faqSection.a2: If you serve EU customers, hire EU employees, or your AI affects EU residents — the AI Act applies extraterritorially, same as GDPR.
faqSection.q3: How is this different from ISO/SOC2?
faqSection.a3: ISO 27001/SOC2 cover information security. EU AI Act covers AI-specific risks: bias, transparency, human oversight. They’re complementary, not overlapping.
faqSection.q4: What if our tool isn’t in the catalog?
faqSection.a4: Add any custom tool manually. Eva AI will interview you and classify risk in approximately 5 minutes.
faqSection.q5: Can we use generated docs for regulatory audits?
faqSection.a5: Yes. All generated documents follow EU AI Act legal requirements. Many customers submit Complior outputs directly to auditors.
faqSection.q6: How long does full setup take?
faqSection.a6: Most companies complete initial setup in 4–6 hours. Classification and training can start immediately after signup.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Choose the plan that fits your AI compliance needs. All paid plans include a 14-day free trial.
pricing.heroSubtitle: All plans include EU AI Act updates, GDPR-compliant hosting, and email support.
pricing.ctaSubtitle: Start free, upgrade when you need more. No credit card required.
pricing.faq1A: Yes. Upgrade instantly, downgrade at next billing cycle. No lock-in contracts on any plan.
pricing.faq2A: Your account converts to the Free plan automatically. No charge unless you explicitly upgrade. All your data is preserved.
pricing.faq3Q: Do you offer discounts for NGOs or startups?
pricing.faq3A: Yes — 30% off Growth and Scale plans for registered NGOs, educational institutions, and startups under 2 years old. Contact us to apply.
pricing.faq4Q: Is my data stored in the EU?
pricing.faq4A: 100%. All data is hosted on Hetzner servers in Frankfurt, Germany. We use only EU-based subprocessors. Full GDPR compliance with DPA available on all plans.
pricing.faq5Q: What payment methods do you accept?
pricing.faq5A: Credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), SEPA direct debit, and bank transfer for annual Enterprise plans. All payments processed via Stripe.
pricing.faq6A: Yes — save 2 months with annual billing (pay for 10, get 12). Toggle the switch above to see annual prices. You can switch between monthly and annual at any time.

quickCheck.title: AI Act Quick Check
quickCheck.subtitle: Does the EU AI Act apply to your company?
quickCheck.q1: Does your company use or deploy AI systems?
quickCheck.q1o1: Yes, we actively use AI tools
quickCheck.q1o3d: Some tools may use AI without you knowing
quickCheck.q2: How many employees does your company have?
quickCheck.q3: Does your company operate in or serve customers in the EU?
quickCheck.q4: What is your primary role with AI systems?
quickCheck.q4o1d: e.g. Using ChatGPT, Copilot, HubSpot AI
quickCheck.q4o2: We build or sell AI products (provider)
quickCheck.q4o2d: e.g. SaaS with ML models, AI APIs
quickCheck.q4o3: Both — we build AI and use third-party AI
quickCheck.q5: Which types of AI tools does your company use?
quickCheck.q5o5d: Document processing, workflow automation, email AI
quickCheck.q5o6d: ML models trained on your company data
quickCheck.q6: What role does AI play in your decision-making?
quickCheck.q6o1d: AI provides insights, humans decide everything
quickCheck.q6o2d: AI recommends, person reviews and approves
quickCheck.q6o4d: Chatbots, AI phone agents, AI recommendations
quickCheck.q7: Do you inform affected persons when AI is used?
quickCheck.q7o1: Yes — we clearly label AI outputs
quickCheck.q7o3: No — users can’t distinguish AI from human
quickCheck.q7o4: Not applicable — AI is internal only
quickCheck.q8: Does your company have an AI usage policy?
quickCheck.q9: Do you use AI in any of these regulated areas?
quickCheck.q9o1d: CV screening, candidate ranking, performance scoring
quickCheck.q9o2d: Credit scoring, risk assessment, pricing models
quickCheck.q9o5d: Legal research AI, case outcome prediction
quickCheck.q10: Does your AI impact individuals’ rights or access to services?
quickCheck.q10o1: Yes — AI influences decisions about people
quickCheck.q10o2: Indirectly — AI informs but doesn’t decide
quickCheck.q11: Is there human oversight of AI outputs?
quickCheck.q11o1d: Documented process, assigned reviewers, override capability
quickCheck.q12: Do you maintain an inventory of all AI systems?
quickCheck.q12o1: Yes — we track all AI tools centrally
quickCheck.q13: Have your employees received AI literacy training?
quickCheck.emailCaptureDesc: Enter your email to receive a personalized compliance roadmap.
quickCheck.resultLowDesc: The EU AI Act has minimal impact on your current operations.
quickCheck.resultMediumDesc: The EU AI Act applies to your company. Action recommended.
quickCheck.resultHighDesc: You have significant compliance obligations under the EU AI Act.

penalty.title: What’s the maximum fine for your company?
penalty.subtitle: Calculate penalties under Art. 99 of the EU AI Act.
penalty.tier1Rule: 7% of worldwide annual turnover or $35M, whichever is higher
penalty.tier1RuleEur: 7% of worldwide annual turnover or €35M, whichever is higher
penalty.tier2Rule: 3% of worldwide annual turnover or $15M, whichever is higher
penalty.tier2RuleEur: 3% of worldwide annual turnover or €15M, whichever is higher
penalty.tier3Rule: 1.5% of worldwide annual turnover or $7.5M, whichever is higher
penalty.tier3RuleEur: 1.5% of worldwide annual turnover or €7.5M, whichever is higher
penalty.smeNote: As an SME with under $10M revenue, proportionate penalties apply under Art. 99(6). Actual fines consider your size, nature, and severity.
penalty.smeNoteEur: As an SME with under €10M revenue, proportionate penalties apply under Art. 99(6). Actual fines consider your size, nature, and severity.
penalty.midNote: Actual fines are determined by national market surveillance authorities and consider factors like severity, duration, prior infringements, and cooperation (Art. 99(7)).

fria.title: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment
fria.subtitle: Art. 27 EU AI Act
fria.notRequired: FRIA is not required for minimal/limited risk tools

onboarding.whatIsFria: A Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA) evaluates how your high-risk AI system may affect people's fundamental rights — including privacy, non-discrimination, and human dignity. It is a mandatory document under the EU AI Act for deployers of high-risk AI.
onboarding.whyRequired: Article 27 of the EU AI Act requires deployers of high-risk AI systems to conduct a fundamental rights impact assessment before putting the system into use.
onboarding.whatHappens: Completing this assessment generates a structured, audit-ready FRIA report (PDF). It also contributes to your overall compliance score and documents your due diligence.
onboarding.urgencyHigh: Required before deployment of this high-risk AI system

general_info.intro: This section captures the basic context of your AI tool deployment. It identifies the tool, its purpose, and the domain in which it operates. Art. 27 requires this context to frame the entire assessment.
general_info.guidance: Most fields are pre-filled from your tool registration. Focus on writing a clear, specific purpose statement that explains exactly how your organization uses this tool — this is the foundation of the entire FRIA.

affected_persons.intro: Identify all groups of people whose fundamental rights may be affected by this AI system. Art. 27 requires you to consider both direct users and those indirectly impacted by AI-driven decisions.
affected_persons.guidance: Think broadly — consider not just direct users but anyone whose rights, opportunities, or access to services may be affected. If vulnerable groups (children, elderly, disabled) are affected, the assessment requirements are stricter.
affected_persons.description: Who is impacted by this AI system

specific_risks.intro: Document the specific risks this AI system poses to fundamental rights. Consider risks related to the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights — particularly privacy (Art. 7-8), non-discrimination (Art. 21), and human dignity (Art. 1).
specific_risks.guidance: For each risk, consider: Which fundamental right is threatened? How severe is the potential impact? How likely is it to occur? Reference specific EU Charter articles where possible (e.g., Art. 21 non-discrimination, Art. 8 data protection).

human_oversight.intro: Describe the human oversight measures in place for this AI system. Art. 14 of the EU AI Act requires that high-risk AI systems are designed to allow effective human oversight during their use.
human_oversight.guidance: Choose the oversight model that best matches your actual practice. Pre-decision oversight offers the strongest protection but may not be practical for all use cases. Document who is responsible and how they can intervene.

mitigation_measures.intro: For each risk identified in the previous section, describe the concrete measures you will take to mitigate it. Art. 27 requires deployers to document proportionate safeguards.
mitigation_measures.guidance: Each mitigation measure should directly address a specific risk. Include who is responsible, what the measure entails, and when it will be implemented. Be specific — 'we will monitor' is too vague; 'monthly bias audit by DPO using tool X' is better.

monitoring_plan.intro: Define how you will continuously monitor the AI system's impact on fundamental rights after deployment. Art. 26(5) requires deployers to monitor system operation and report incidents.
monitoring_plan.guidance: Choose a review frequency proportionate to the risk level. Define measurable metrics (e.g., false positive rates, demographic parity). Assign a responsible team and describe how findings feed back into the mitigation measures.

description.label: Description of Impact

category.label: Risk Category
category.placeholder: e.g. Privacy, Non-discrimination, Human dignity, Freedom of expression...

riskDescription.label: Risk Description
riskDescription.description: Describe the specific risk to this fundamental right
riskDescription.placeholder: e.g. The AI scoring model may produce biased outcomes for candidates from certain ethnic backgrounds...

severity.label: Severity
severity.description: How severe would the impact be if this risk materializes

likelihood.label: Likelihood
likelihood.description: How likely is this risk to occur

concurrent.label: Concurrent

escalationProcess.description: How issues are escalated when the AI system produces problematic outputs

risk.label: Related Risk
risk.description: Which fundamental rights risk does this measure address

measure.label: Mitigation Measure
measure.description: Describe the specific action taken to reduce this risk
measure.placeholder: e.g. Quarterly bias audit of scoring outcomes by demographic group, conducted by external auditor...

responsible.label: Responsible Person
responsible.description: Who is accountable for implementing this measure

deadline.label: Implementation Deadline
deadline.description: When this measure must be in place

frequency.label: Review Frequency
frequency.description: How often the AI system's fundamental rights impact is reviewed

metrics.label: Key Metrics
metrics.description: Measurable indicators used to track the AI system's impact on fundamental rights
metrics.placeholder: e.g. False positive rate, demographic parity index, complaint count, override frequency...

responsibleTeam.label: Responsible Team
responsibleTeam.description: The team or role responsible for ongoing monitoring
responsibleTeam.placeholder: e.g. Compliance Team, Data Protection Office...

reviewProcess.label: Review Process
